hardPrerequisiteProof.label: Phase 2 trust contract, capability gates, and identity authority rules
NHS login auth transaction and local session contracts
Patient linkage, calibrated match model, and optional PDS seam

mergeGates.label: Shared-contract merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Prevents parallel tracks from redefining protected identity, session, grant, or telephony readiness contracts.
exitRules: No duplicate owner for AuthTransaction, Session, PatientLink, IdentityBinding, AccessGrant, or TelephonyEvidenceReadinessAssessment.
Every sibling consumer imports the shared seam instead of copying schema locally.
All new PARALLEL_INTERFACE_GAP_PHASE2_* entries are either resolved or explicitly owned.

mergeGates.label: Security and masking merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Keeps raw auth claims, phone identifiers, audio refs, and evidence out of logs, URLs, DOM attributes, and operational rows.
exitRules: Raw identity evidence and telephony identifiers write only through evidence-vault ports.
Audit and telemetry fields use the seq174 masking allow-list.
Provider raw payloads remain quarantined at adapter boundaries.

mergeGates.label: Runtime/publication merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Separates simulator-first delivery from later live provider onboarding while preserving production-shaped contracts.
exitRules: Mock-now providers remain contract-faithful and may not add shortcut semantics.
Live provider assumptions are captured in the mock/live provider matrix.
Publication and route tuples remain fail-closed when stale or missing.

mergeGates.label: Request-convergence merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Forces auth, grant, ownership, telephony readiness, and phone ingress to converge through canonical intake and authority-settled binding.
exitRules: Telephony can promote only from safety_usable and ready_to_promote readiness.
Request.patientRef and Episode.patientRef derive only from current IdentityBinding.
Continuation and claim redemption use canonical AccessGrant service semantics.

mergeGates.label: Browser-surface and accessibility merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Protects later patient portal, continuation, repair, and status surfaces from diagram-only or inaccessible contract drift.
exitRules: Every board/diagram has adjacent table or list parity.
Keyboard traversal, reduced motion, responsive layout, and landmark coverage are verified.
Patient-facing copy never promises authority beyond current session/grant/readiness posture.

parallelInterfaceGaps.closure: Tasks 170-173 are hard prerequisites with checklist status and validator commands recorded.
Shared interface seams are registered before any task 175-194 can claim an authority boundary.
Mock-now providers and live-provider onboarding assumptions are split in a machine-readable matrix.
Every track row states capability ownership, shared packages, authority boundary, merge gates, and allowed parallel neighbors.
Telephony caller verification, evidence-vault writes, readiness, continuation, and request convergence are explicit cross-track touchpoints.

forbiddenParallelActions: No duplicate redefinitions of IdentityBinding, PatientLink, AuthTransaction, Session, AccessGrant, or telephony readiness schemas.
No sibling task may write another authority-owned persistence table or command handler.
No raw identity evidence, raw auth claims, phone numbers, caller identifiers, handset proofs, recordings, or transcript payloads outside the evidence vault or audio quarantine boundary.
No private redirect, grant, or continuation-token model may bypass PostAuthReturnIntent, RouteIntentBinding, AccessGrant, AccessGrantScopeEnvelope, or TelephonyContinuationEligibility.
No telephony routine promotion before TelephonyEvidenceReadinessAssessment(usabilityState=safety_usable, promotionReadiness=ready_to_promote).
integrationTouchpoints: Auth callback writes raw claims through evidence-vault ports before binding/session decisions.
PatientLinkDecision feeds IdentityBindingAuthority but never writes binding truth.
SessionGovernor consumes settled binding, capability, and return-intent posture before issuing a local session.
Telephony caller verification writes captured identifiers through the evidence vault and submits only binding intents.
TelephonyContinuationEligibility feeds canonical AccessGrant issuance for seeded/challenge SMS continuation.
Telephony convergence enters canonical intake only through request-convergence gates and current readiness verdict.
